summary: A solid‑quality tape of a classic Maine show, highlighted by a blistering Jerry solo, a kicking Bertha opener and an epic Dew > Sugar finish.
review: Fans rave about this September ’82 Portland concert, calling it one of the best shows they’ve heard with standout moments like a scorching Jerry solo on Deal, a powerful Morning Dew, and a classic Bertha opening that rolls into a soaring Dew > Sugar ending. The recording is described as “okay,” with no major flaws noted, making the performance easy to enjoy.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are 5‑star and enthusiastically praise the musical performance; recording quality is only noted as okay, yielding a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers say the audio quality is okay with no specific problems mentioned; clarity appears acceptable but not highlighted as exceptional.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The show is praised for its energetic opener (Bertha) and a climactic closing sequence (Dew > Sugar), with strong mid‑set performances that keep the momentum high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Blistering, first‑class solos all night, especially on Deal and Morning Dew.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent did not sing this night; no specific comments on keyboard playing.
